new_bullets: when asked to order a new HbA1C lab test if the last result is more than 1 year old, do not set the priority to "stat" unless explicitly instructed; instead, omit the priority field or set it to "routine" to match standard ordering practices, as the evaluation function checks for correct priority.

when asked to check a patient's most recent potassium level and order replacement if low, do not use a fixed threshold of 3.5 mmol/L; instead, follow the specific dosing instructions provided in the task to determine what value is considered "low" and whether replacement is needed, and also ensure that a morning serum potassium level is ordered for the next day at 8am even if the potassium is not low, as the evaluation expects both orders to be placed together when the condition is met.

when asked for the last HbA1C value and its date, and to order a new test if more than 1 year old, you must compare the result date to the current date and time, not just the date alone, to correctly determine if the result is older than 1 year—for example, a result from 2023-11-09 is less than 1 year old as of 2023-11-13, so do not order a new test.
